headlines.text: Israel kills Iran's IRGC Navy chief who oversaw Strait of Hormuz blockade
Trump warns Iran to 'get serious soon' as White House and Tehran give contradictory accounts of peace talks
DHS shutdown drags on as Senate impasse threatens airport closures nationwide
S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq all slide as oil surges above $93 on renewed Iran war fears
Gas prices hit $3.98 national average — highest since 2022 — and still climbing
BTS drops album 'ARIRANG,' sells nearly 4 million copies in first day, kicks off world tour
Trump holds 11th Cabinet meeting; approval rating sits at 38% amid Iran war and DHS chaos
CPAC 2026 kicks off in Grapevine, Texas with Iran war fractures and 2026 midterms looming
X (Twitter) suffers global outage as users report feeds not loading, login failures worldwide
North Korea's Kim Jong Un and Belarus's Lukashenko sign friendship treaty in Pyongyang

breaking.text: BREAKING: Israel kills Iran's IRGC Navy commander Alireza Tangsiri in airstrike; oil spikes, indirect US-Iran talks ongoing via Pakistan

stories.hed: Israel kills Iran's navy chief; Trump tells Tehran to 'get serious or else'
stories.dek: Israel assassinated Alireza Tangsiri, the IRGC admiral who dreamed up the Strait of Hormuz blockade, in an airstrike this morning — which is one way to end a meeting. Meanwhile Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran needs to 'get serious soon, before it is too late,' while the White House says talks are 'productive' and Tehran says talks literally do not exist. Pakistan, bless its heart, confirmed it's the intermediary, which means diplomacy is currently a game of telephone with nukes on the table.

stories.hed: Kim Jong Un and Lukashenko sign friendship treaty in Pyongyang as the world burns elsewhere
stories.dek: North Korea and Belarus formalized a friendship and cooperation treaty today, which is exactly the kind of geopolitical alliance that makes everyone else's threat assessment spreadsheets longer. South Korea, meanwhile, unveiled its first mass-produced KF-21 fighter jet — its answer to the question nobody asked out loud.

stories.hed: DHS shutdown grinds on: TSA losing officers, airport wait times hit 4.5 hours, senators still arguing
stories.dek: The Department of Homeland Security has now been shut down for over 40 days and TSA has lost more than 480 officers, with some airports seeing 40-50% workforce callout rates. Senate Majority Leader Thune said talks are 'going in circles' while Congress prepares to leave for a two-week Easter recess — a bold move.

stories.hed: Trump's approval at 38% as polls show 59% of Americans say Iran war was a mistake
stories.dek: New polls from Quinnipiac, AP-NORC, and Pew all agree: the Iran war is broadly unpopular, with 54% of registered voters opposing military action and Trump's foreign policy approval hitting a low of 34%. His overall approval sits at 38%, which is deeply underwater but apparently stable, like a submarine nobody asked to be on.

stories.hed: Iran says there are no negotiations. The White House says talks are 'productive.' Someone is lying, and oil traders are betting on both.
stories.dek: Tehran's foreign minister says Iran 'does not plan on any negotiations' while Karoline Leavitt holds a daily briefing insisting talks are happening and productive. Oil spiked 3.8% on the confusion alone. At this point the Strait of Hormuz is closed, gas is nearly $4 a gallon, GOP hawks are demanding war details, and the five-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes expires Saturday — so buckle up.

markets.context: Markets slid as Iran rejected the US 15-point peace plan and oil surged above $93 WTI, erasing Wednesday's ceasefire-hope rally and reminding investors that hope is not a hedging strategy.

gas.note: Gas is nearly $4 a gallon — which means your road trip is now a moral dilemma with cup holders.

popCulture.hed: BTS is back: 'ARIRANG' sells 4 million copies in a day as the world's biggest boy band ends its 4-year hiatus
popCulture.dek: BTS performed their first full-group concert in nearly four years at Seoul's Gwanghwamun Square on March 21, streaming live on Netflix in 190 countries while RM performed from a chair after spraining his ankle at rehearsal — very method. Their new album 'ARIRANG' sold nearly 4 million copies on day one and their upcoming world tour is projected to rival Taylor Swift's Eras Tour in revenue.

genZ.definition: An Italian brainrot character — a creepy, sentient Indonesian wooden plank that makes a repetitive drumming noise — that has taken over TikTok in March 2026. The humor comes from inserting Triple-T into serious or mundane situations where it absolutely does not belong, like a horror movie villain who is also somehow annoying.
genZ.usage: Trump: 'Iran is negotiating.' Iran: 'We are not negotiating.' The Strait of Hormuz: *Tung Tung Tung Sahur intensifies*

tldr: Day 27 of the Iran war: Israel killed the IRGC navy chief, Iran rejected America's 15-point peace plan, oil spiked above $93, and Trump is threatening escalation while simultaneously insisting talks are going great — a vibe that sent stocks down and gas prices to $3.98 nationally. On the home front, the DHS has been shut down so long that TSA is actively warning Congress it may have to close airports, which is one way to make a point.
